channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.nintendolife.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.nintendolife.com

item.title: Poll: Box Art Brawl: Duel - Diddy Kong Racing
item.content:encoded:
Monkey business

We are back for another edition of Box Art Brawl, and it's a good one this week.

Last time, to celebrate all things Sonic for his 35th anniversary, we matched up every Sonic game on Switch, leading to a beefy nine poll options! Sonic Origins Plus came out on top with 33% of the vote, Sonic Mania Plus landed in second with 20%, and Sonic Superstars took the bronze with 15%. Coincidentally, those are the three games featured in the newly announced Classic Sonic Collection.

We're celebrating another big anniversary this week, as the almighty N46 turns 30. To mark the occasion, we're finally turning to one of the very best games on the system, Diddy Kong Racing. Gosh, what we'd give to finally see another entry in this Rare racing series, eh?

There are just two covers to choose between this week, so let's check 'em out.

North America / Europe

The European design added its classic black border, but it shares the exact same art as North America. And it's a good 'un. Diddy Kong himself is flying up front, while his racing companions bring up the rear. It's even got Wizpig firing out a lightning bolt! Uuuuunlimited powahhhhh.

Japan

The Japanese design uses the region's portrait formatting to add a bit more verticality to proceedings. This time, DK flies over the title art, with far more characters crammed in behind him — hi, Conker and Banjo! There's no lightning bolt, but it certainly feels a touch more kinetic.

Thank you for voting! We'll see you next week for another edition of Box Art Brawl!

Jim came to Nintendo Life in 2022 and, despite his insistence that The Minish Cap is the best Zelda game and his unwavering love for the Star Wars prequels (yes, really), he has continued to write news and features on the site ever since.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/features/poll-box-art-brawl-duel-diddy-kong-racing
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Mina The Hollower Is Joining Rivals Of Aether II As A Guest Fighter In 2027
item.content:encoded:
A console release is also on the way

Yacht Club's new game Mina The Hollower has already established itself with millions of sales and it seems the renowned Hollower is already gearing up for her next adventure.

Rivals creator Dan Fornace has announced Mina will be the first guest character in Rivals of Aether II. She'll be joining the game at some point in 2027. No other information about this DLC character has been shared just yet.

Although Rivals of Aether II isn't available on Nintendo's platforms, according to the game's roadmap, there are plans to release console versions of the title in 2028. This game launched on PC in 2024 and swapped the first game's pixel visuals for 3D graphics.

The original Rivals of Aether made its debut on the Switch eShop in 2020. If you haven't played it, it's an indie platform fighter drawing inspiration from the Smash Bros. series.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/06/mina-the-hollower-is-joining-rivals-of-aether-ii-as-a-guest-fighter-in-2027
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Multiple Switch Games Have Received Switch 2 Compatibility Fixes
item.content:encoded:
Shovel Knight and more

The Switch 2 is now a year into its lifecycle, and Nintendo and other developers continue to improve the compatibility of Switch titles on the new hybrid system.

With this in mind, another batch of compatibility updates have recently gone live. Improvements have reportedly been made to Shovel Knight and various other titles. Here's the full list of recent compatibility fixes via Nintendo Everything:

Compatibility Fixes

  • Artifact Seekers
  • Landing Hero Haneda×787
  • Raiden IV x Mikado Remix
  • RICO
  • Shovel Knight: Treasure Trove
  • Skateboard Drifting with Maxwell Cat: The Game Simulator
  • Strania - The Stella Machina EX

You can find out more about compatibility fixes on Nintendo's official compatibility search page. Nintendo has also reportedly identified more unsupported titles and some other Switch games with audio issues on Switch 2:

  • Drakkar Crew - Unsupported
  • KIYO - Bunny Tyranny - Unsupported
  • Bar Stella Abyss (Japan release) – audio issues
  • Monster Menu: The Scavenger’s Cookbook – audio issues

This follows a bunch of compatibility updates last month for titles such as Monster Hunter Stories and Wolfenstein: Youngblood. The Switch 2 also recently got a system firmware update.

Have you played any of these games on the Switch 2? What other Switch titles would you like to see updated? Let us know in the comments.

[source nintendoeverything.com]

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/06/multiple-switch-games-have-received-switch-2-compatibility-fixes
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Granblue Fantasy Versus: Rising Announced For Switch 2, Out September
item.content:encoded:
Alongside the next character DLC

Cygames and Arc System Works have this week announced the fighting game Granblue Fantasy Versus: Rising is on its way to the Switch 2.

This version of the title will be launching alongside the new 2.60 game update on 17th September 2026. Here's the reveal:

The update will include a new DLC character "Id", new stage, new system mechanic, adjustments to the system mechanics, character adjustments, new costumes and more.

Here's what Push Square had to say about the PS5 version of this game when it released in 2023:

"Rising is the game that the original Granblue Fantasy Versus should have been, and while it's a bit cheeky that players are being asked to pay for it all over again — with yet more DLC characters incoming — it's hard to argue with the overall package. This is a gorgeous fighter that feels great to play."

When more details are shared, we'll provide an update.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply
